summary: Strong opening set; Rain & Shakedown shine, second half uneven
blurb: The first set bursts with Rain, Shakedown Street and Cold Rain & Snow, delivering tight grooves and fiery guitar work. Later songs like Estimated Prophet and Eyes Of The World lose momentum, giving the second half a flatter feel. Highlights include China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and Days Between.
review: September 16, 1993 at Madison Square Garden opened with a punchy four‑song surge—Rain into Shakedown Street, then Wang Dang Doodle and Cold Rain & Snow—that many reviewers cite as the night’s high point. Jerry’s tone was “funky and perfect” early on, Vince Welnick was in rare top form, and the band rode the momentum through Althea and Eternity before a gentle close with Don’t Ease Me In. The second set started strong with the classic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue and an energetic Estimated Prophet, but the ensuing Eyes Of The World, Drum/Space stretch, and later numbers felt “flat” or “over‑indulgent,” leaving the energy waning until the final burst of Days Between and a brief encore of Liberty. For listeners seeking the clearest capture of that opening surge while tolerating an uneven second half, the Miller soundboard (gd1993-09-16.sbd.miller.94226.sbeok.flac16) remains the recommended source.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Reviews are mixed: strong praise for the opening set and specific songs (Rain, Shakedown Street, China > Rider) but consistent notes of a flat or boring second half. This yields a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard capture that clearly presents the standout opening set and overall performance, making it the most reliable listening source.

key_highlights: Opening four‑song run (Rain → Shakedown Street → Wang Dang Doodle → Cold Rain & Snow) is praised for its energy.
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue shines in the second set.
Vince Welnick’s keyboard work is highlighted as rare top form throughout the show.
Second half loses momentum, with Eyes Of The World and Drum/Space described as flat.

band_performance.Jerry: Funky, perfect tone on the early set and energetic solos, but his acoustic tone later sounds sterile and less compelling.
band_performance.Vince: Delivers rare top‑form keyboard work, adding sparkle to both sets.
band_performance.Mickey: Drum segment described as vapid and over‑indulgent, lacking energy.
band_performance.Billy: Same as Mickey; the percussion feels flat during the later Drum/Space stretch.
